findings.note: Attach-after-shell remains in waiting-to-reconnect/no-debuggee state and does not produce a live kernel debuggee.
KD can reach a kernel-connected state during guest restart, but transport degrades into breakin/communication errors.
CDB did not improve the VMware serial transport path; current runs failed before producing usable logs.

A focused serial-config matrix reached kernel-connected guest-restart sessions in 4/4 variants and transport_ok in 3/4 variants, narrowing the blocker to reproducible command/breakin roundtrips rather than generic attach failure.
A direct breakin-once probe on the strongest current guest-restart serial base kept kernel connectivity and shell health, but still failed to produce a real breakin/command roundtrip.
A thinner queued-command roundtrip probe proved that post-restart commands can resume, but they resumed only after a fatal system error break and required snapshot recovery.

A focused start-order matrix across bonc/none/b break policies and 3/10/20-second attach leads preserved kernel connectivity and shell recovery in every run, but none of the six variants produced a post-restart queued command roundtrip.

A reconnect-command matrix using host-side breakin-once injection across the same bonc/none/b and 3/10/20-second lead variants also ended 6/6 as attach_ok_command_not_executed, with one fatal-break outlier and zero breakin successes.

recommended_next_actions: Keep WinDbg single-key arbitration paused until a guest-restart profile can keep kernel connectivity and process command/breakin roundtrips reproducibly.
Treat both queued roundtrip and host-side reconnect command injection as characterized on the current guest-restart kd base.
Move next to pipe endpoint or debugger launch-mode variants, not more frontend churn.
Treat attach-after-shell as a no-debuggee observation path, and guest-restart as the current transport base candidate.
